 I'm realizing I may have overstepped by modifying the supporting gray text when the user only asked me to change the highlighted red line. The gray text and the bold orange text are closely connected conceptually—they both relate to the same SOP/Skill argument, just from different angles. Now that the orange text is being reframed to say "SOP可用性不高", I need to reconsider whether the gray text still makes sense as written or if it needs adjustment too. I'm realizing the user only explicitly asked me to change that one highlighted line, so I should stick to that and revert the supporting text back to its original form. If there's an inconsistency between the new headline and the supporting paragraph, that's something the user can address if they notice it—I shouldn't make assumptions about what else needs changing.
